news.headline: Form 144: RPID insider notifies sale of 10,000 Class A shares (option exercise)
news.summary: An RPID insider filed a Form 144 to sell 10,000 Class A shares obtained via option exercise, continuing a string of small routine insider sales also dated June 10 (5,000 shares), June 22 (3,688 shares) and July 1 (6,312 shares), against roughly 41.7 million shares outstanding.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Announces Inducement Grant Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
news.summary: The company granted 60,000 restricted stock units to a newly hired employee as a material inducement to employment, vesting in three equal annual installments over three years. This remains the company's most recent official press release as of July 19.

news.headline: KeyBanc Capital Markets cuts Rapid Micro Biosystems price target to $5 from $8
news.summary: KeyBanc lowered its price target on RPID to $5 from $8, citing the dilutive impact of the May equity raise, while other desks (Stifel, and KeyBanc's own underlying rating) kept a positive Overweight/Buy stance on the growth thesis.

news.headline: Lake Street cuts Rapid Micro Biosystems price target to $7 from $8
news.summary: Lake Street Capital Markets, a joint bookrunner on the May offering, trimmed its price target to $7 from $8 following the pricing of the new share/warrant issuance.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Announces Pricing of Up to Approximately $32 Million Underwritten Offering
news.summary: RPID priced Class A shares with Series A/B warrants at a combined $1.955, raising about $9.8M in immediate gross proceeds (up to $32M if all warrants are later exercised for cash); Bain Capital Life Sciences, Armistice Capital and company insiders, including director Kirk Malloy, participated in the deal.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue rose 11% YoY to $8.0M, with consumable/recurring revenue up nearly 30%, while EPS of -$0.31 missed consensus; management guided Q2 revenue to roughly $7.8M and reaffirmed full-year 2026 targets of 30-38 Growth Direct placements and $37-41M revenue.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (analyst-calendar estimate, not yet company-confirmed)
catalysts.detail: Earnings-calendar trackers (TradingView, MarketBeat, Zacks) still had no company-confirmed Q2 report date as of July 19; the company typically issues its advance scheduling press release about one week ahead (it did so on May 6 for the May 13 Q1 date), so a formal date announcement is expected in late July. Historically RPID has reported Q2 in early-to-mid August (Aug 4, 2023; Aug 12, 2025). Watch the guided ~$7.8M Q2 revenue, new Growth Direct placement cadence toward the 30-38 unit FY26 target, consumables pull-through/recurring revenue growth, and cash runway following the May equity raise and subsequent sell-side price-target cuts.

pulse: News flow has been very quiet since the mid-May dilution event: the last operational press release remains the June 10 inducement-grant filing, and the only fresh company-specific item through July 19 is a continuing string of small, routine insider Form 144 option-exercise sales (June 10, June 22, July 1, July 8). The May offering (~$9.8M upfront, up to $32M if warrants are exercised) brought in Bain Capital, Armistice Capital and insider buying, but KeyBanc and Lake Street both cut price targets ($5 and $7 from $8) on the added share count, even as both firms kept constructive ratings. The stock has traded roughly flat-to-down through mid-July (around $2.21 on July 15, still off double digits since March per market trackers), with no new orders, partnerships, or guidance updates disclosed since Q1. The underlying business is still executing on Growth Direct placements (Q1 revenue +11% YoY, recurring revenue +30%, FY26 guidance of 30-38 placements / $37-41M revenue reaffirmed), so the story remains a race between placement/consumables growth and continued cash burn and dilution ahead of the next quarterly report, expected in early-to-mid August but not yet formally scheduled.
